description: Implement ADR-2026-04-17-1000: add a hermetic regression test asserting `hex analyze` rejects cross-adapter, ports→adapter, and domain→adapter imports. Closes the last gap in docs/EVIDENCE.md §1 — the rejection path of the boundary analyzer becomes automated rather than a 3-step manual procedure.

phases.description: Create hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/ with a minimal hex-shaped Rust project: domain/mod.rs (empty), ports/mod.rs (imports domain only), adapters/primary/cli.rs and adapters/secondary/db.rs (each imports ports only), main.rs as composition root, Cargo.toml stub. Fixture is the baseline that all five test cases mutate from.

gate.command: test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/Cargo.toml && test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/src/domain/mod.rs && test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/src/ports/mod.rs && test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/src/adapters/primary/cli.rs && test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/src/adapters/secondary/db.rs && test -f hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/src/main.rs

phases.name: Phase 1 — author hermetic fixture

tasks.description: Author the 6-file minimal hex-layout fixture under hex-cli/tests/fixtures/boundary-violation/ exactly as specified in ADR-2026-04-17-1000 §Test shape. domain/mod.rs is empty; ports/mod.rs has only `use crate::domain::*;`; primary/cli.rs and secondary/db.rs each `use crate::ports::*;` only; main.rs is the composition root that pulls in all four layers; Cargo.toml is a [package] stub sufficient for `hex analyze` to walk the tree (no build needed).
tasks.name: Create boundary-violation fixture tree

phases.description: Author hex-cli/tests/analyze_rejects_violation.rs with five cases: baseline_passes, cross_adapter_import_rejected, ports_imports_adapter_rejected, domain_imports_adapter_rejected, baseline_passes_after_revert. Each case copies the fixture into a tempfile::tempdir(), applies its mutation (or none), invokes the hex binary via the same hex_bin() helper pattern as hex-cli/tests/reconcile_evidence.rs, and asserts on exit code + stderr substrings (file stem + layer name, per ADR §Mitigations). Each violation case re-runs baseline_passes first to catch fixture corruption.
phases.name: Phase 2 — write the regression test

tasks.description: Implement the 5 #[test] functions matching the ADR's table. Reuse the hex_bin() resolution logic from reconcile_evidence.rs (target/debug/hex → target/release/hex → which hex). Use tempfile::tempdir() per test for isolation. Match stderr on stable tokens only — the file stem (`primary/cli.rs`, `ports/mod.rs`, `domain/mod.rs`) and the layer name (`adapters/secondary`) — NOT whole error strings.
tasks.name: Author analyze_rejects_violation.rs with 5 test cases

phases.description: Run the new integration test locally and confirm: 4 violation cases reject (non-zero exit, expected stderr tokens), 1 baseline case passes, total wall time under 5s. If any case fails, root-cause whether the fixture, the test assertions, or the analyzer itself is wrong before adjusting.
phases.name: Phase 3 — local verification

tasks.description: Execute `cargo test -p hex-cli --test analyze_rejects_violation` and confirm 5 passed / 0 failed. Capture timing — must stay under 5s on a reference dev box per ADR §CI wiring. Failure here means either the fixture is wrong (P1) or the test assertions are wrong (P2); fix at the source.
tasks.name: Run analyze_rejects_violation locally and verify all 5 cases

phases.description: Inspect .github/workflows/ci.yml and confirm the existing `cargo test --workspace` step picks up the new test without any workflow edit (ADR §CI wiring states no new job needed). If the workspace step does not include hex-cli's tests/ dir, escalate — do NOT add a new CI job; that's a separate ADR.
phases.name: Phase 4 — confirm CI auto-pickup

tasks.description: Read .github/workflows/ci.yml. Confirm `cargo test --workspace` (or an equivalent step that includes hex-cli) is present. No edits expected. If absent, halt and report — adding CI infrastructure is out of scope for this ADR.
tasks.name: Verify ci.yml workspace test step covers the new file

phases.description: Replace the manual 3-step `cd examples/hex-weather && echo … && hex analyze . && git checkout` block in docs/EVIDENCE.md §1 with the canonical reproducer `cargo test -p hex-cli --test analyze_rejects_violation`. Keep the manual sequence below it as a human-readable illustration.
phases.name: Phase 5 — update EVIDENCE.md

tasks.description: Edit docs/EVIDENCE.md §1 (Tree-sitter hexagonal boundary analyzer). Insert the `cargo test -p hex-cli --test analyze_rejects_violation` invocation as the authoritative reproducer ABOVE the existing manual block. Demote the manual block to an illustrative human-readable sequence.
tasks.name: Cite the automated test as primary reproducer in EVIDENCE.md §1
